rulings.Case Title: NICHOLAS WALKER et al VS. PLATINUM CAPITAL GROUP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Aurora Loan Services' Motion For Summary Judgment Or Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 15, 2013, line 8 - Defendant Aurora Loan Services' Motion For Summary Judgment Or Summary Adjudication. Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment is GRANTED. Defendant's objections to Plaintiff's evidence are sustained. Plaintiffs concede that their causes of action are based solely on alleged wrongdoings during modification. Plaintiffs fail to submit any admissible evidence to create triable issue of material fact. Plaintiff does not present any evidence that Defendants were making false representations or that foreclosure occurred while modification was still under review. Plaintiff's request for continuance is DENIED. Ramos declaration is devoid of any support for CCP 437c(h). =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION AS SUCCESSOR VS. STEPHANIE NAIFEH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Reconsideration On Order On Def'Ts Mtn To Remove Clerk'S Default; Decl
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 15, 2013, line 1 - DEFENDANT STEFANIE NAIFEH's Motion For Reconsideration of Order denying Defendant's Motion To Remove Clerk'S Default. Defendant Naifeh's Motion for Reconsideration of Order Denying Defendant's Motion to Remove Clerk's Default is DENIED. Defendant failed to sustain her burden under CCP 1008. No new facts pertaining to denial of untimely motion to Set Aside the Default. =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION AS SUCCESSOR VS. STEPHANIE NAIFEH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions [Ccp 128.7];
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 15, 2013, line 1 - DEFENDANT STEFANIE NAIFEH's Motion For Sanctions. Defendant's Motion for Sanctions under CCP 128.7 is OFF CALENDAR in light of ruling on motion for reconsideration. =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER BUTLER VS. LINDA LINGREN et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 15, 2013, line 2 - DEFENDANT LINDA LINGREN's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant's Demurrer to First Amended Complaint is OVERRULED. FAC sufficiently alleges all elements of IIED in the context of landlord-tenant relationship. See paragraph 9, 10, 11, 12, 15, 16. Counsel for the moving party repeatedly fails to comply with SFLR 2.6B and C. See CUD-13-644454; CUD-12-643746. No courtesy copies of the First Amended Complaint and Reply were provided to Department 501 and/or 302. =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: RWW PROPERTIES LLC et al VS. SHELDON FONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: An Order To Show Cause Why Defendant Sheldon Fong Should Not Be Found In Contempt Of The Stipulated Order Entered On March 12, 2013
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 15, 2013, line 3 - Plaintiffs RWW Properties LLC and HPROF LLC's Motion for An Order To Show Cause Why Defendant Sheldon Fong Should Not Be Found In Contempt Of The Stipulated Order Entered On March 12, 2013. Plaintiffs' Motion for Order to Show Cause why Defendant Fong should not be held in Contempt of Court. Hearing Required. Defendant is ordered to provide courtesy copy of his opposition immediately. Defendant's counsel is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6B in future. =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. VS. BERNETTA ADOLPH et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 15, 2013, line 4 - DEFENDANT BERNETTA ADOLPH's Motion To Vacate Judgmnt. Defendant's Motion to Vacate Judgment is continued to May 17, 2013 to be heard by Honorable Lynn O'Malley-Taylor in Department 611 at 9:00 a.m. =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: AL DE LORENZI VS. JON ZUCKMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Forfeiture Due To Hardship;
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 15, 2013, line 5 - DEFENDANT JON ZUCKMAN's Motion For Relief From Forfeiture Due To Hardship. Defendant's Motion for Relief from Forfeiture. Hearing Required. Moving party is ordered to provide the Court with courtesy copies of its moving papers immediately. Counsel for the moving party repeatedly fails to comply with SFLR 2.6B. See CUD-13-643869 =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: QUINCY YU et al VS. JEFFREY YU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Vacate Voluntary Dismissal And Enter Dismissal With Prejudice Ordering That Deft Is Th Prevailing Party And Awarding Deft Costs Including Attorney Fees
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 15, 2013, line 6 - DEFENDANT JEFFREY YU's Motion To Vacate Voluntary Dismissal And Enter Dismissal With Prejudice Ordering That Deft Is Th Prevailing Party And Awarding Deft Costs Including Attorney Fees. Defendant's Motion to Vacate Voluntary Dismissal and Enter Dismissal with Prejudice Ordering that Defendant is the prevailing party and warding Defendant costs and attorney's fees is OFF CALENDAR, untimely per CCP 1005. Defendant's attorney is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6B in future. =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: S&L REALTY VS. BOL TIANGSING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Set Aside Default Judgment And Underlying Default
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 15, 2013, line 9 - DEFENDANT BERNARDO TIANGSING's Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment And Underlying Default. Defendant¿s Motion to Set Aside Default is GRANTED. Defendant shall post $3,482.25 and file his answer no later than May 15, 2013 4 p.m. =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: RITA L. STEIN VS. SIMON HALTEH et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 15, 2013, line 7 - DEFENDANT SIMON HALTEH, VERA ARBID's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants' Demurrer to First Amended Complaint is sustained with leave to amend to address discrepancy between allegation in the "3 Day Notice" and in paragraph 6 of FAC. Additionally, Plaintiff argues in opposition that rental agreement as alleged in paragraph 6(a) and (b) was later amended, however, no such allegation is made in the Complaint. See paragraph 6(d), rendering FAC and Notice uncertain. =(501/AJR)
